rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash for the clearer description 'Verula Ordinaria. Brought over —'. For the pence amount, visually confirmed a small '1' rather than a '0' upon close inspection, aligning with gpt-5-mini's interpretation.
Amounts are in full consensus between both extractors. Chose gemini-flash's description 'Gas Lights 1/4 to Dec 1850' as a more accurate reading of the handwritten '1/4' abbreviation.
Amounts are in full consensus. Chose gpt-5-mini's description 'Do. Mich.' as it better represents the visual '..' preceding 'Mich.', which commonly denotes 'Ditto' in ledgers.

rows.description: Mr. Falconer 1/2 Sel. on £247.14.2, 3/4 C.E. to July 1850
rows.notes: Amounts are in full consensus. Chose gemini-flash's description as it is a much more complete and accurate transcription of the verbose entry, especially the name and details of the transaction.

Added as a header row based on visual inspection. The text 'Subscriptions, &c.' appears as a clear section label with no associated amount on its line. gemini-flash did not separate this into a header. gpt-5-mini correctly identified a header but included 'Viz. Infirmary' in its description, which belongs to the subsequent entry.

Visually identified as the first entry under the 'Subscriptions, &c.' header. Used gpt-5-mini's amount and type, but refined description to accurately include 'Viz.' which is clearly present. gemini-flash misread this description entirely as 'Sir Grenan'.
Full consensus on description and amounts.

Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Kidlington' is a more accurate reading of the description than 'Malinton' (gemini-flash), by examining the initial letter which is a clear stylized 'K', agreeing with gpt-5-mini.
Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Newington' (gemini-flash) is a more accurate reading of the description than 'Kensington' (gpt-5-mini) based on the letter shapes.

Amounts are in full consensus. Chose gemini-flash's description 'Little Few Do'. Visually, 'Few' is a plausible reading, though 'Tew' as suggested by gemini-flash is also possible, indicating a degree of ambiguity.
Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Garsington' (gemini-flash) is a more accurate reading than 'Garston' (gpt-5-mini) due to the clearly visible 'ing' formation.
Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Merton' (gemini-flash) is a more accurate reading than 'Morton' (gpt-5-mini), as the second letter is clearly an 'e'.
Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Garsington' (gemini-flash) is a more accurate reading than 'Carrington' (gpt-5-mini), as the initial letter is clearly a 'G'.
Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Withenham' (gemini-flash) is a more accurate spelling than 'Wittenham' (gpt-5-mini), due to the clear 'h' formation.
Full consensus on description and amounts. gpt-5-mini noted a slight ambiguity in spelling ('Bennington' vs 'Bensington') which is not strongly apparent on visual inspection; 'Bensington' is clear.

Amounts are in full consensus. 'Gwinker' (gemini-flash) appears slightly more accurate than 'Gurnker' (gpt-5-mini) upon close visual inspection of the 'w' formation, though the word remains ambiguous as noted by both extractors.

Amounts are in full consensus. 'Poor of Gwinker' chosen for consistency with the previous entry's place name, which upon re-evaluation is the more plausible reading here as well, despite some fading in the script as noted by gpt-5-mini. Aligns with gemini-flash's reading.
Full consensus on description and amounts.

rows.description: Small Livings in Diocese of Oxford
rows.notes: Amounts are in full consensus. Visually combining the best parts of both descriptions: 'Livings' (from gpt-5-mini, as the plural 's' is visible) and 'of Oxford' (from gemini-flash) provides the most accurate full description.
Full consensus on amounts and substantial description. Selected gemini-flash's punctuation for 'Do' (with a period), which appears more consistent with the ledger's style.
Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed the plural 's' for 'Marshalls', aligning with gemini-flash.

Amounts are in full consensus. 'Bele Ringer' from gemini-flash is a better visual fit than 'Pole Ringer' from gpt-5-mini. The note from gemini-flash ('Bele' is likely 'Bell') shows careful consideration of the script.
Visually confirmed that the pounds column is blank for 'Corn Prices', aligning with gemini-flash's explicit note of 'No pounds amount given'. gpt-5-mini erroneously included '1' for pounds.
Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed the plural 's' for 'Offerings', aligning with gpt-5-mini.
Full consensus on amounts. Chose gpt-5-mini's description style (using parentheses and a semicolon) as a clearer representation of the parenthetical text in the original manuscript.
Full consensus on description and amounts.
Visually confirmed the pounds amount is '7', aligning with gpt-5-mini. gemini-flash misread this as '4'. Chose gemini-flash's em-dash style for the description.

Visually confirmed the pounds amount is '7', aligning with gpt-5-mini. gemini-flash misread this as '4'. Chose gemini-flash's concise '2nd Do' description as it accurately represents the 'ditto' style of the ledger.
Full consensus on amounts. Chose gemini-flash's concise '3rd Do' description, which is consistent with the ledger's 'ditto' notation.
Full consensus on amounts. Chose gemini-flash's concise '4th Do' description, consistent with the ledger's 'ditto' notation.

Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Trustees'' is a more accurate reading of the description than 'Stubbs' (gemini-flash), aligning with gpt-5-mini, whose note also accurately describes the script's ornateness.

Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Mr. Kempe' is the correct name (aligning with gpt-5-mini) rather than 'Mr. Hempton' (gemini-flash). Adopted gemini-flash's em-dash style and capitalization of 'Viz.' for the description.

Amounts are in full consensus. Visually confirmed 'Mr. Pilgrims' is the correct name, aligning with gemini-flash, and omitting the repeated 'Open Scholarships' as this is an indented sub-entry. gpt-5-mini's reading of 'Wilges' is incorrect.
Amounts are in full consensus. 'Mr. Roase' is confirmed visually, aligning with gemini-flash, and omitting the repeated 'Open Scholarships' for this indented sub-entry.
Full consensus on description, type, and amounts. The double-ruled line visually confirms this as a total.
